name: Cheese Rarebit (Usda)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap. Allow enough extra wrap to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans for food to be served without freezing.
2. Melt butter in a large saucepan. Stir in flour and seasonings. Add hot milk slowly. Cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
3. Add cheese; stir until cheese is melted.
4. To serve without freezing: Serve over hot toast.
5. To freeze:
- Pour each part to be frozen into a baking pan.
- Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature.
- Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over the top of the food. Fold edges together and fold several times so paper lies directly on top of the food. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape.
- Label with name of food, date of freezing, and last date the food should be used for best eating quality (about 6 months).
- Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from the pans.
6. To heat frozen food:
- Preheat oven to 350° F. (moderate).
- Remove freezer wrap. Place food in a baking pan.
- Heat 45 minutes or until bubbly at edges. Stir occasionally during heating.
- After heating, stir until rarebit is smooth. If rarebit is too thick, thin with hot milk. Serve over hot toast.
